name: CRM/ERP Fitment
summary: Turn your workflows and constraints into a clear requirements snapshot that makes shortlisting easy and defensible.
outcomes: Integration touchpoints mapped (ERP/accounting, website, WhatsApp, BI)
whatThisIs: A structured discovery focused on how your business actually runs.
We capture processes, approvals, reporting KPIs, data rules, and integration needs.
Output is a clear fitment document that becomes the base for shortlisting and demos.
deliverables: Data rules (ownership, required fields, quality checks)
Fitment summary (what matters most, what to avoid)
inputsNeeded: Your process steps (high level is enough)

faqs.q: Do we need to know technical details?
faqs.a: No. You explain your workflows and outcomes. We translate them into requirements.
Yes. Fitment is based on your needs and constraints, not sponsorships.

name: Shortlist + Evaluation Pack
summary: Get a shortlist (3–5 options) with trade-offs, a demo script, and a scorecard so every tool is evaluated fairly.
whatThisIs: A structured shortlisting and evaluation kit based on your fitment inputs.
Instead of feature comparisons, we focus on fit, implementation reality, and long-term ownership.
deliverables: Trade-offs summary (what you gain vs what you give up)
Scorecard (weighted criteria based on your priorities)
Avoid if rules for each option
bestFor: Teams stuck between too many tools
Teams running repeated demos without clarity
Leaders who need a decision they can defend internally
howItWorks: Optional: join 1–2 demos to keep them structured
inputsNeeded: Your priorities (speed vs governance vs ecosystem alignment)

faqs.q: Will you recommend one tool only?
faqs.a: Usually we provide 3–5 best-fit options. If a clear winner exists, we'll state it and why.
We provide TCO signals and effort bands. Exact pricing is confirmed during vendor discussions.

name: Implementation Partner Match
summary: Get introductions to implementation partners aligned to your platform choice, scope, industry, and timeline.
whatThisIs: We focus on delivery capability, industry fit, integration experience, and support model.
deliverables: Partner shortlist aligned to your scope
Partner due diligence checklist (references, delivery model, team composition)
Scope framing prompts to avoid scope creep
bestFor: Teams that don't want to gamble on implementation quality
Projects with integrations or multi-team complexity
howItWorks: Match partners by capability and geography/timezone
Help compare proposals using a common checklist
timeline: Partner intros can start within 2–5 days.

faqs.a: No. These are introductions to relevant firms. Final contracting remains between you and the partner.

name: Independent Oversight (Optional)
summary: Add a governance layer during implementation to protect outcomes: scope control, QA checkpoints, and adoption readiness.
whatThisIs: A light oversight layer that keeps implementation aligned to outcomes.
We don't replace the partner—we reduce risk and improve adoption.
deliverables: Milestone plan and sprint governance checks
Role-based adoption plan (training + change management basics)
bestFor: High-risk projects (many stakeholders, heavy workflows, integrations)
Teams with limited internal CRM/ERP ownership
Implementations where adoption is a known risk

faqs.a: No—done right it reduces rework and accelerates decisions.
